messages.content:
Service domain: linear.app
Corpus: 0 scraped page(s) below. Every URL you emit MUST appear in this corpus.

Map every credential and surface this service exposes, applying the quality rules. Output the JSON object only.

(the corpus is empty — return empty credentials and surfaces)

summary.description: One-line overview of the service's integration surface.

id.description: Short stable id you reference from surface auth via use[].id, e.g. 'resend_api_key'.

generateUrl.description: The page (from the corpus) where the user MINTS the credential. Null for CLI-login creds acquired by a command.

setup.description: Markdown: a human acquisition guide written around the EASIEST path. Where to go, what to click, gotchas. If a CLI login acquires it, the guide is 'run `x login`' — NOT an OAuth authorize/token endpoint walkthrough. Write EVERY URL as a markdown link [label](https://…); put literal values (header names, token prefixes, scopes, commands) in `backticks`.

fields.description: Named sub-secrets for an inherently multi-part credential (compound / GitHub-App appId+privateKey / basic email+token). Null for a single-secret credential. (An array here; the caller keys it into a record.)

credentials.description: Global credential registry (each item carries its own `id`). Define each credential ONCE even if many surfaces accept it. May be empty.

spec.description: MACHINE-READABLE OpenAPI doc URL (a pointer, never inlined). Must end in .json/.yaml/.yml or contain openapi/swagger. A docs portal is NOT a spec — leave null.

url.description: Base URL — when there's no spec, or not derivable from the spec.

name.description: Display name (NOT identity — the caller assigns the slug).

signal.description: A re-verifiable machine signal the service publishes (e.g. 'openapi:securitySchemes', 'oauth-protected-resource'). Rare from a scrape — prefer 'discovered'.

evidence.description: Doc URL(s) from the corpus that confirm this. Every URL MUST appear in the corpus.

basis.description: How we learned a thing exists. From a scrape you almost always emit `discovered` with the corpus URLs you read.

signal.description: A re-verifiable machine signal the service publishes (e.g. 'openapi:securitySchemes', 'oauth-protected-resource'). Rare from a scrape — prefer 'discovered'.

evidence.description: Doc URL(s) from the corpus that confirm this. Every URL MUST appear in the corpus.

basis.description: How we learned a thing exists. From a scrape you almost always emit `discovered` with the corpus URLs you read.

anyOf.description: Confirmed PUBLIC — no credential needed. `basis.evidence` MUST cite corpus URL(s) that say so.

scheme.description: The OpenAPI securityScheme NAME this one credential satisfies.

in.description: Where the credential rides. Default header.

scheme.description: HTTP auth scheme prefix, e.g. 'Bearer'.

command.description: A command that ACQUIRES/binds the credential, e.g. 'mint login', 'wrangler login'.

env.description: Env var(s) that carry the credential, e.g. ['RESEND_API_KEY'].

mechanics.description: How a credential binds to a surface. Use 'cli' when a login command acquires it; 'http' when it rides the request; 'spec' when an OpenAPI scheme names it.

use.description: Credentials sent TOGETHER (AND), each with its own placement. One element = a single credential.

signal.description: A re-verifiable machine signal the service publishes (e.g. 'openapi:securitySchemes', 'oauth-protected-resource'). Rare from a scrape — prefer 'discovered'.

evidence.description: Doc URL(s) from the corpus that confirm this. Every URL MUST appear in the corpus.

basis.description: How we learned a thing exists. From a scrape you almost always emit `discovered` with the corpus URLs you read.

entries.description: OR alternatives — at least one is needed.

anyOf.description: Auth not determinable from the corpus (NOT the same as public).

auth.description: A surface's auth requirement. `none` requires publicEvidence; never leave a required surface with empty entries — use `unknown` instead.

requiredHeaders.description: Mandatory NON-auth headers (a version pin, a required User-Agent).

name.description: A token substituted wherever {name} appears in the surface url.

in.description: Default 'url'.

url.description: The GraphQL endpoint (a schema has no endpoint of its own).

name.description: Display name (NOT identity — the caller assigns the slug).

signal.description: A re-verifiable machine signal the service publishes (e.g. 'openapi:securitySchemes', 'oauth-protected-resource'). Rare from a scrape — prefer 'discovered'.

evidence.description: Doc URL(s) from the corpus that confirm this. Every URL MUST appear in the corpus.

basis.description: How we learned a thing exists. From a scrape you almost always emit `discovered` with the corpus URLs you read.

signal.description: A re-verifiable machine signal the service publishes (e.g. 'openapi:securitySchemes', 'oauth-protected-resource'). Rare from a scrape — prefer 'discovered'.

evidence.description: Doc URL(s) from the corpus that confirm this. Every URL MUST appear in the corpus.

basis.description: How we learned a thing exists. From a scrape you almost always emit `discovered` with the corpus URLs you read.

anyOf.description: Confirmed PUBLIC — no credential needed. `basis.evidence` MUST cite corpus URL(s) that say so.

auth.description: A surface's auth requirement. `none` requires publicEvidence; never leave a required surface with empty entries — use `unknown` instead.

basis.description: How we learned a thing exists. From a scrape you almost always emit `discovered` with the corpus URLs you read.

entries.description: OR alternatives — at least one is needed.

anyOf.description: Auth not determinable from the corpus (NOT the same as public).

auth.description: A surface's auth requirement. `none` requires publicEvidence; never leave a required surface with empty entries — use `unknown` instead.

requiredHeaders.description: Mandatory NON-auth headers (a version pin, a required User-Agent).

name.description: A token substituted wherever {name} appears in the surface url.

in.description: Default 'url'.

url.description: The MCP connect endpoint (NOT a docs page).

name.description: Display name (NOT identity — the caller assigns the slug).

signal.description: A re-verifiable machine signal the service publishes (e.g. 'openapi:securitySchemes', 'oauth-protected-resource'). Rare from a scrape — prefer 'discovered'.

evidence.description: Doc URL(s) from the corpus that confirm this. Every URL MUST appear in the corpus.

basis.description: How we learned a thing exists. From a scrape you almost always emit `discovered` with the corpus URLs you read.

signal.description: A re-verifiable machine signal the service publishes (e.g. 'openapi:securitySchemes', 'oauth-protected-resource'). Rare from a scrape — prefer 'discovered'.

evidence.description: Doc URL(s) from the corpus that confirm this. Every URL MUST appear in the corpus.

basis.description: How we learned a thing exists. From a scrape you almost always emit `discovered` with the corpus URLs you read.

anyOf.description: Confirmed PUBLIC — no credential needed. `basis.evidence` MUST cite corpus URL(s) that say so.

scheme.description: The OpenAPI securityScheme NAME this one credential satisfies.

in.description: Where the credential rides. Default header.

scheme.description: HTTP auth scheme prefix, e.g. 'Bearer'.

command.description: A command that ACQUIRES/binds the credential, e.g. 'mint login', 'wrangler login'.

env.description: Env var(s) that carry the credential, e.g. ['RESEND_API_KEY'].

mechanics.description: How a credential binds to a surface. Use 'cli' when a login command acquires it; 'http' when it rides the request; 'spec' when an OpenAPI scheme names it.

use.description: Credentials sent TOGETHER (AND), each with its own placement. One element = a single credential.

items.description: One integration surface. Emit ONLY the types that actually exist — no empty placeholder surfaces.

surfaces.description: Typed surface inventory. Empty when the service has no public integration surface.
